description_long: This object consists of a rectangular, flat, dark-colored base with three evenly spaced, raised, pill-shaped blisters aligned in a row. Each blister is semi-transparent and appears to contain a single round or oval tablet, typical of pharmaceutical blister packs. The base has a smooth, slightly reflective surface, while the blisters are more translucent and slightly frosted. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, with no visible branding or text on the surface. The object is likely made from a combination of plastic and foil, commonly used for medicine packaging. The blisters are designed to be pressed from the back to release the tablets, providing both protection and easy access.
description: A rectangular blister pack with three semi-transparent pill blisters on a dark, smooth base, each containing a single tablet. The pack is compact and unbranded, typical for medicine packaging.
description_view_0: Front view showing three pill blisters aligned horizontally on a flat base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the raised profile of the three blisters above the flat base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat base with the three pill blisters visible as protrusions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blisters' height and alignment on the base.
